findings.location: Stage 4 — Monitoring SLA vs Performance Characteristics
findings.description: Stage 4 SLA states '99.5% of events delivered within 60 seconds' while Performance section states 'sub-60-second latency for 95th-percentile events.' Different percentiles (99.5th vs 95th) for the same 60-second latency threshold — either intentional (requiring explanation) or an unintentional inconsistency.
findings.evidence: Stage 4: "99.5% of events delivered within 60 seconds of ingestion under nominal load." Performance: "sub-60-second end-to-end latency for 95th-percentile events from ingestion acknowledgment to data warehouse availability."
findings.suggestion: Align percentiles or add a clarifying note distinguishing SLA commitment (99.5th) from benchmark measurement (95th).

findings.description: Overview states monitoring runs continuously in parallel, but Stage 4 is numbered sequentially with Stages 1–3, implying sequential positioning.
findings.evidence: Overview: "Monitoring runs continuously in parallel with all stages." Architecture section: Stage 1, Stage 2, Stage 3, Stage 4 — sequential numbering.
findings.suggestion: Rename to 'Continuous Monitoring' or 'Monitoring Layer' to clarify its parallel nature.

findings.description: The specific bottleneck driving the 3× throughput differential for sub-10MB payloads is not named, leaving the claim underdeveloped for an architecture brief.
findings.evidence: "batch processing achieves 3× higher throughput than record-by-record streaming for payloads under 10MB due to reduced per-record overhead." The bottleneck type (connection overhead, serialization, etc.) is unspecified.
findings.suggestion: Name the specific bottleneck: e.g., 'due to reduced per-connection establishment overhead' or 'due to reduced serialization round-trips.'

findings.description: The 'dedicated overflow delivery channel' is named but its operational properties (retry behavior, SLA alignment with primary path) are not described.
findings.evidence: "routed to the same Stage 3 delivery path via a dedicated overflow delivery channel." No further description.
findings.suggestion: Add a sentence clarifying whether overflow channel shares retry policy and SLA with the primary path.
